$description: Whitelist of known approximations where Rust and TS are expected to diverge by design. Read by Phase D parity harness auto-triage. Source: KNOWN_APPROXIMATION_REFERENCE_DRAFTS in src/pages/referenceContent.ts.

entries.allowed_divergence: HP/status rollback uses 9s snapshot with 25% max HP cap, not simple direct heal. Skip winner/ttk deltas when Rewind fires on either side.
Auto-arms at HP <= 25%, explodes ~1s later. Outdated approximation. Skip deltas when selfDestructProfile set.
Target assumed caught immediately on activation. Stand-and-fight simplification.
Frosty applies only +25% regen in Compare. Weather side not modeled.
BB forces Burn+8 outcome, Compare may roll or force. Skip when Bad Omen fires and BB/Compare modes selected differ.
Crit chance → constant multiplier. Chance effects → expected avg stacks. Already implemented symmetrically in both engines.
First Hunker activation is immediate. 5s delay applies only to re-activations.
Target treated as permanently inside puddle for full duration.
Target does not try to destroy Totem. Poison ticks continue for full duration.
Only Tranquility side modeled. Madness side absent from both engines.
Compare starts with 5 Defensive stacks, no decay. Treated as permanent buff.
Compare-only approximation for server-driven passive ticking. Shifts first tick timing.
Blocked regen ticks are buffered, apply after anti-heal ends.
Pack Healer nearby applies regen to both sides in Compare, not one-sided.
Treated as durationless stat bonuses in Compare. No in-fight duration timer.
Both creatures forced to fixed bite cooldown. Not a full air-combat simulation.

Rust applies 1 Burn + 1 Fear per breath tick (per Reference: 'inflicts Burn Status Probability=100% STACKS=1 and Fear Status Probability=100% STACKS=1'). TS applies 2 Burn + 1 Fear per tick due to a regex bug in parseBreathAilments (runtimeHelpers.ts:54): the hardcoded Spirit Glare path in breathSpecialRuntime.ts adds Burn+Fear, and the generic applyBreathAilments path re-adds Burn (but fails to match Fear because the regex prefix group `(?:^|[,:]|\binflicts?\b)` does not match the 'and' conjunction before 'Fear Status'). Rust is spec-correct; TS is buggy. Fixture expected values for Spirit Glare matchups are regenerated from Rust.
